title: Reverse Proxies, Forwarded Trust, Message Framing, and Smuggling Defense · Go Semantics Case: Framing Smuggling Consistency
summary: A gateway preserves client Forwarded fields, uses deprecated ReverseProxy.Director, disagrees with an upstream about malformed framing, forwards hop-by-hop fields, and constructs unsafe backend queries. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo framing smuggling consistency increment in proxy rewrite and parser-consistency threat model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Prevent request smuggling by rejecting ambiguous Content-Length and Transfer-Encoding, malformed whitespace, conflicting parsers, oversized fields, and unsafe downgrade paths per RFC 9112.
Strip untrusted forwarding fields, rebuild them only at trusted hops, constrain backend targets, remove hop-by-hop state, and prevent header spoofing.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Framing Smuggling Consistency · Theory Framing Smuggling Consistency
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 3807 in a bursting intake service near its concurrency ceiling. Build deterministic Go evidence to reject ambiguous framing and deprecated Director-style proxy mutation; then remove one trusted intermediary and recompute authority without forwarded fields. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Prevent request smuggling by rejecting ambiguous Content-Length and Transfer-Encoding, malformed whitespace, conflicting parsers, oversized fields, and unsafe downgrade paths per RFC 9112.
